conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 and lives with me here in Birmingham. I was wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me first read the official eligibility rules to ensure I provide you with accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the official guidance, I need to ask you a few questions about Alex to determine your eligibility.

Since Alex is 17, I need to know: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, attending school, college, A-levels, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He finished his GCSEs last year and decided not to continue with sixth form or college. He's been taking some time to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment. He's just been at home, trying to decide what direction he wants to go in.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own name, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not receiving any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me explain the situation based on the official rules:

Since Alex is 17 years old, the key factor is whether he is in approved education or training. According to the UK government rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

However, since Alex:
- Is 17 years old (over 16)
- Is not currently in approved education or training
- Finished his GCSEs last year and did not continue with sixth form or college

Unfortunately, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex under these circumstances. Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training.

**However**, there is one important exception: If Alex were to register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services after leaving education, you could apply for Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks while he is registered.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that based on the current circumstances, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

If Alex's circumstances change - for example, if he decides to return to education, starts an approved training course, or registers with a government-sponsored careers service - your eligibility could change. In that case, you may want to contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which means he is over the age of 16. According to UK Child Benefit rules, for children aged 16 or over, the claimant normally still qualifies for Child Benefit if the child is under 20 AND stays in approved education or training. Alex finished his GCSEs last year and is not currently in sixth form, college, or any approved training. He is not working and is not receiving benefits in his own name. Since Alex is not in approved education or training, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. (Note: If Alex were to register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, Child Benefit could continue for up to 20 weeks, but there is no indication this has occurred.)
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and lives with the claimant in Birmingham. While Alex is under 20, he is not currently in approved education or training, which is a requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.
